app.title: Bousai Map
app.subtitle: Multilingual disaster-prevention info, leaving no one behind

home.heroTitle: Be ready for disasters — the map for everyone
home.heroSubtitle: A multilingual disaster-prevention portal for living safely in Japan: earthquakes, tsunamis, typhoons and more
home.situationBody: Japan sits where four tectonic plates meet and is home to many of the world's active volcanoes. People live alongside earthquakes, tsunamis, volcanic eruptions, typhoons and heavy rain. The right knowledge and everyday preparation save lives when disaster strikes.
home.featureMapDesc: Find the nearest shelters and hazard zones on the map
home.featureKnowledgeDesc: Learn about earthquakes, tsunamis, volcanoes and how to prepare
home.featureAiDesc: Ask about disaster prevention in plain language

knowledge.heading: Disaster Info
knowledge.lead: An introduction to the natural disasters common in Japan and how to prepare for them.
knowledge.tipsTitle: Key points to prepare and act

earthquake.name: Earthquake
earthquake.summary: Earthquakes large and small happen often in Japan; strong shaking can collapse buildings and topple furniture.
tips: Protect yourself first: Drop, Cover, Hold On
After the shaking stops, turn off fire sources and secure an exit
Fix furniture in place and check evacuation routes with your family

tsunami.name: Tsunami
tsunami.summary: If you feel strong or long shaking near the coast, a tsunami may be coming.
tips: Evacuate immediately to high ground or a tsunami evacuation building
Stay away from the sea when a tsunami warning is issued
Go higher and farther — do not return until it is confirmed safe

volcano.name: Volcano
volcano.summary: Japan has many active volcanoes; eruptions bring flying rocks, pyroclastic flows and ashfall.
tips: Follow eruption warning levels and access restrictions
Carry a helmet and mask when hiking volcanoes
During ashfall, protect your eyes, nose and mouth with goggles and a mask

typhoon.name: Typhoon
typhoon.summary: Typhoons approach from summer to autumn, bringing violent winds, heavy rain and storm surges.
tips: Check the latest weather and evacuation information frequently
Put away outdoor items that could blow away
Stay away from rivers and the sea, and evacuate early while it is light

flood.name: Flood
flood.summary: Heavy rain and typhoons can cause rivers to overflow and urban areas to flood.
tips: Check your home's risk level on the hazard map in advance
Move to higher ground early (going to upper floors also helps)
Avoid underpasses, basements and swollen channels

shelters.heading: Evacuation Shelters
shelters.dataSource: Source: GSI "Designated Emergency Evacuation Site Data" (Kyoto City)
